namespace {

// Map table names to their corresponding struct types.
const std::map<std::string, rfl::named_tuple_t> table_struct_map = {
    {"Student", Student},
    {"Course", Course},
    {"Score", Score},
    {"Major", Major}
};

template <class T>
const std::string get_table_name() {
  using Type = std::remove_cvref_t<T>;
  const auto& it = table_struct_map.find(rfl::type_name_t<Type>().str());
  if (it == table_struct_map.end()) {
    throw std::runtime_error("Unknown table type.");
  }
  return it->first;
}

template <class T>
const std::vector<std::pair<std::string, std::string>>> get_columns() {
  using Type = std::remove_cvref_t<T>;
  const auto columns = rfl::get_named_tuple_t<Type>().fields() |
      transform([](const auto& field) -> std::pair<std::string, std::string> {
        return {field.name(), field.type_name()};
      }));
  return columns;
}

template <class T>
const std::optional<std::string> get_primary_key() {
  using Type = std::remove_cvref_t<T>;
  const auto pk = rfl::get_named_tuple_t<Type>().fields() |
      filter([](const auto& field) -> bool {
        using FieldType = typename field::Type;
        return std::is_class_v<std::remove_cvref_t<FieldType>> &&
           std::is_aggregate_v<std::remove_cvref_t<FieldType>> &&
           std::remove_cvref_t<FieldType>::is_primary_key;
      }) |
      transform([](const auto& field) { return field.name(); }));
  return pk;
}

}  // namespace

template <class T>
void add_eas_records(const std::vector<std::vector<std::optional<std::string>>>& _data) {
  const auto table_name = get_table_name<T>();
  const auto columns = get_columns<T>();

  std::stringstream stream;
  stream << "INSERT INTO " << table_name << " ("
       << sqlgen::join(", ",
                         columns | transform([](const auto& _col) { return _col.first; })))
       << ") VALUES ("
       << sqlgen::join(", ",
                         columns | transform([](const auto&) { return "?"; })))
       << ");";

  auto conn = sqlite::connect(db_path).value();
  auto stmt = conn->prepare(stream.str()).value();

  for (const auto& row : _data) {
    if (row.size() != columns.size()) {
      throw std::runtime_error("Row has incorrect number of columns.");
    }
    std::vector<std::optional<std::string>> params;
    for (size_t i = 0; i < columns.size(); ++i) {
      params.push_back(row[i]);
    }
    stmt->execute(params).transform([](const auto&) { /* success */ });
  }
}

void modify_eas_records_by_id(const std::string& _table_name,
                              const int _record_id,
                              const std::string& _col_name,
                              const std::optional<std::string>& _new_value) {
  const auto it = table_struct_map.find(_table_name);
  if (it == table_struct_map.end()) {
    throw std::runtime_error("Table not found.");
  }
  using T = it->second;

  const auto pk = get_primary_key<T>();
  if (!pk) {
    throw std::runtime_error("Primary key not found.");
  }

  const auto columns = get_columns<T>();
  const auto col_it = columns | find_if([](const auto& _c) { return false; }, [&](const auto& _c) { return _c.first == _col_name; }));
  if (col_it == columns.end()) {
    throw std::runtime_error("Column not found.");
  }

  std::stringstream stream;
  stream << "UPDATE " << _table_name << " SET " << _col_name << " = "
       << sqlgen::to_sql(_new_value) << " WHERE " << pk << " = "
       << std::to_string(_record_id) << ";";

  auto conn = sqlite::connect(db_path).value();
  conn->execute(stream.str()).transform([](const auto&) { /* success */ });
}

void delete_eas_records_by_id(const std::string& _table_name,
                              const int _record_id) {
  const auto it = table_struct_map.find(_table_name);
  if (it == table_struct_map.end()) {
    throw std::runtime_error("Table not found.");
  }
  using T = it->second;

  const auto pk = get_primary_key<T>();
  if (!pk) {
    throw std::runtime_error("Primary key not found.");
  }

  std::stringstream stream;
  stream << "DELETE FROM " << _table_name << " WHERE " << pk
       << " = " << std::to_string(_record_id) << ";";

  auto conn = sqlite::connect(db_path).value();
  conn->execute(stream.str()).transform([](const auto&) { /* success */ });
}

namespace test_design_eas_database {

struct Student {
  std::string SNo;
  std::string SName;
  int Sex = 1;
  std::optional<std::string> Birthday;
  std::optional<int> Major;
  float Scorses = 0.0f;

};

  int CID;
  std::string CName;

  std::optional<int> CTime;
  float Credit = 0.0f;
};

struct Score {
  std::string SNo;
  int CID;
  std::optional<float> Score;
};

void design_eas_database() {
  const auto db_path = "test_db.sqlite";
  const auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect(db_path);

  // Create Student table
  const auto student_table = sqlgen::dynamic::Table{
      .name = "Student",
      .columns = {
          sqlgen::dynamic::Column{.name = "SNo", .type = sqlgen::dynamic::types::VarChar{.length = 10},
                                    .constraints = {sqlgen::dynamic::constraints::NotNull{},
                                    sqlgen::dynamic::constraints::PrimaryKey{}}};
          sqlgen::dynamic::Column{.name = "SName", .type = sqlgen::dynamic::types::VarChar{.length = 30},
                                    .constraints = {sqlgen::dynamic::constraints::NotNull{}}};
          sqlgen::dynamic::Column{.name = "Sex", .type = sqlgen::dynamic::types::Int{},
                                    .constraints = {sqlgen::dynamic::constraints::Nullable{}},
                                    .default_value = "1"};
          sqlgen::dynamic::Column{.name = "Birthday", .type = sqlgen::dynamic::types::DateTime{},
                                    .constraints = {sqlgen::dynamic::constraints::Nullable{}}};
          sqlgen::dynamic::Column{.name = "Major", .type = sqlgen::dynamic::types::Int{},
                                    .constraints = {sqlgen::dynamic::constraints::Nullable{}}};
          sqlgen::dynamic::Column{.name = "Scorses", .type = sqlgen::dynamic::types::Float{},
                                    .constraints = {sqlgen::dynamic::constraints::Nullable{}},
                                    .default_value = "0"};
          sqlgen::dynamic::Column{.name = "Remark", .type = sqlgen::dynamic::types::Text{},
                                    .constraints = {sqlgen::dynamic::constraints::Nullable{}}};
      }
  };
  conn->execute(student_table.to_create_table_statement());

  // Create Course table
  const auto course_table = sqlgen::dynamic::Table{
      .name = "Course",
      .columns = {
          sqlgen::dynamic::Column{.name = "CID", .type = sqlgen::dynamic::types::Integer{},
                                    .constraints = {sqlgen::dynamic::constraints::NotNull{},
                                    sqlgen::dynamic::constraints::PrimaryKey{}},
                                    .auto_incr = true},
          sqlgen::dynamic::Column{.name = "CName", .type = sqlgen::dynamic::types::VarChar{.length = 30},
                                    .constraints = {sqlgen::dynamic::constraints::NotNull{}}};
          sqlgen::dynamic::Column{.name = "Term", .type = sqlgen::dynamic::types::Int{},
                                    .constraints = {sqlgen::dynamic::constraints::Nullable{}},
                                    .default_value = "1"};
          sqlgen::dynamic::Column{.name = "CTime", .type = sqlgen::dynamic::types::Int{},
                                    .constraints = {sqlgen::dynamic::constraints::Nullable{}}};
          sqlgen::dynamic::Column{.name = "Credit", .type = sqlgen::dynamic::types::Float{},
                                    .constraints = {sqlgen::dynamic::constraints::NotNull{}},
                                    .default_value = "0"};
      }
  };
  conn->execute(course_table.to_create_table_statement());

  // Create Score table
  const auto score_table = sqlgen::dynamic::Table{
      .name = "Score",
      .columns = {
          sqlgen::dynamic::Column{.name = "SNo", .type = sqlgen::dynamic::types::VarChar{.length = 10},
                                    .constraints = {sqlgen::dynamic::constraints::NotNull{}}};
          sqlgen::dynamic::Column{.name = "CID", .type = sqlgen::dynamic::types::Int{},
                                    .constraints = {sqlgen::dynamic::constraints::NotNull{}}};
          sqlgen::dynamic::Column{.name = "Score", .type = sqlgen::dynamic::types::Float{},
                                    .constraints = {sqlgen::dynamic::constraints::Nullable{}}};
      },
      .primary_key = sqlgen::dynamic::PrimaryKey{
          .columns = {"SNo", "CID"}
      },
      .foreign_keys = {
          sqlgen::dynamic::ForeignKey{.column = "SNo", .references = sqlgen::dynamic::Reference{.table = "Student", .column = "SNo"}},
          sqlgen::dynamic::ForeignKey{.column = "CID", .references = sqlgen::dynamic::Reference{.table = "Course", .column = "CID"}}
      }
  };
  conn->execute(score_table.to_create_table_statement());
}

}  // namespace test_design_eas_database

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <optional>
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>
#include <chrono>
#include <iomanip>
#include <sstream>

namespace query_active_users_posts {

struct User {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> user_id;
  std::string username;
};

struct Post {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> post_id;
  int user_id;
  sqlgen::DateTime created_at;
};

struct ActiveUser {
  static constexpr const char* tablename = "ActiveUserView";
  static constexpr bool is_view = true;
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> user_id;
  std::string username;
};

std::vector<ActiveUser> query_active_users_posts() {
  auto conn = sqlgen::SQLiteConnection("test_db.sqlite");
  
  // Create User and Post tables
  conn.create_table<User>();
  conn.create_table<Post>();
  
  // Define date range
  const auto start_date = sqlgen::DateTime{2025, 12, 1};
  const auto end_date = sqlgen::DateTime{2025, 12, 31};
  
  // Execute the query
  const auto query = conn.from<User>("u")
                        .join<Post>("p", "u.user_id = p.user_id")
                        .where("p.created_at >= ? AND p.created_at <= ?", start_date, end_date)
                        .select("u.user_id", "u.username")
                        .distinct();
  
  return query.value();
}
}  // namespace query_active_users_posts
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <optional>
#include <rfl.hpp>
#include <string>
#include <vector>

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>

#include "query_course_enrollment_stats.hpp"

namespace query_course_enrollment_stats {

struct Course {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> course_id;
  std::string title;
};

struct Enrollment {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> enrollment_id;
  sqlgen::ForeignKey<int, Course, "course_id"> course_id;
  std::optional<float> grade;
};

struct CourseStats {
  std::string title;
  int student_count;
  std::optional<double> avg_grade;
};

std::vector<CourseStats> query_course_enrollment_stats() {
  const auto conn = sqlgen::connect<sqlgen::SQLite>("test_db.sqlite");

  // Create Course table.
  const auto create_course_table = sqlgen::to_sql<Course>().create_as();
  conn->execute(create_course_table);

  // Create Enrollment table.
  const auto create_enrollment_table = sqlgen::to_sql<Enrollment>().create_as();
  conn->execute(create_enrollment_table);

  // Execute the query.
  const auto query = R"(
    SELECT c.title, COUNT(e.enrollment_id) AS student_count, AVG(e.grade) AS avg_grade
    FROM Course c
    LEFT JOIN Enrollment e ON c.course_id = e.course_id
    GROUP BY c.course_id, c.title;
  )";
  const auto res = conn->execute(query);

  // Process the results.
  std::vector<CourseStats> stats;
  for (const auto& row : res) {
    CourseStats cs;
    cs.title = row["title"].as<std::string>();
    cs.student_count = row["student_count"].as<int>();
    cs.avg_grade = row["avg_grade"].as<std::optional<double>>();
    stats.emplace_back(std::move(cs));
  }

  return stats;
}

}  // namespace query_course_enrollment_stats
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>
```cpp
#include <ranges>
#include <rfl.hpp>
#include <rfl/json.hpp>
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

namespace query_customer_purchase_history {

struct Customer {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> customer_id;
  std::string name;
  std::string email;
};

struct Product {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> product_id;
  std::string name;
  double price;
};

struct Order {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> order_id;
  sqlgen::ForeignKey<int, Customer, "customer_id"> customer_id;
  sqlgen::Timestamp<"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"> order_date;
  double total_amount;
};

struct OrderItem {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> item_id;
  sqlgen::ForeignKey<int, Order, "order_id"> order_id;
  sqlgen::ForeignKey<int, Product, "product_id"> product_id;
  int quantity;
};

struct PurchaseHistory {
  std::string product_name;
  sqlgen::Timestamp<"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"> order_date;
  int quantity;
};

std::vector<PurchaseHistory> query_customer_purchase_history(int customer_id) {
  try {
    auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");
    conn.create_as<Customer>();
    conn.create_as<Order>();
    conn.create_as<OrderItem>();
    conn.create_as<Product>();

    const auto query_str =
        "SELECT p.name AS product_name, o.order_date AS order_date, oi.quantity AS quantity "
        "FROM Customer c "
        "JOIN \"Order\" o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id "
        "JOIN OrderItem oi ON o.order_id = oi.order_id "
        "JOIN Product p ON oi.product_id = p.product_id "
        "WHERE c.customer_id = ? "
        "ORDER BY o.order_date DESC";

    auto query = conn->prepare_statement(query_str);
    query.bind_int(1, customer_id);

    auto res = query.execute().transform([](auto&& _stmt) {
      return _stmt.to<std::vector<PurchaseHistory>>();
    });

    return res.value_or(std::vector<PurchaseHistory>());

  } catch (const std::exception& e) {
    return {};
  }
}
}  // namespace query_customer_purchase_history
</answer>

<answer>
```cpp
#include <ranges>
#include <rfl.hpp>
#include <rfl/json.hpp>
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

namespace query_customer_purchase_history {

struct Customer {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> customer_id;
  std::string name;
  std::string email;
};

struct Product {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> product_id;
  std::string name;
  double price;
};

struct Order {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> order_id;
  sqlgen::ForeignKey<int, Customer, "customer_id"> customer_id;
  sqlgen::Timestamp<"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"> order_date;
  double total_amount;
};

struct OrderItem {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> item_id;
  sqlgen::ForeignKey<int, Order, "order_id"> order_id;
  sqlgen::ForeignKey<int, Product, "product_id"> product_id;
  int quantity;
};

struct PurchaseHistory {
  std::string product_name;
  sqlgen::Timestamp<"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"> order_date;
  int quantity;
};

std::vector<PurchaseHistory> query_customer_purchase_history(int customer_id) {
  try {
    auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");
    conn.create_as<Customer>();
    conn.create_as<Order>();
    conn.create_as<OrderItem>();
    conn.create_as<Product>();

    const auto query_str =
        "SELECT p.name AS product_name, o.order_date AS order_date, oi.quantity AS quantity "
        "FROM Customer c "
        "JOIN \"Order\" o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id "
        "JOIN OrderItem oi ON o.order_id = oi.order_id "
        "JOIN Product p ON oi.product_id = p.product_id "
        "WHERE c.customer_id = ? "
        "ORDER BY o.order_date DESC";

    auto query = conn->prepare_statement(query_str);
    query.bind_int(1, customer_id);

    auto res = query.execute().transform([](auto&& _stmt) {
      return _stmt.to<std::vector<PurchaseHistory>>();
    });

    return res.value_or(std::vector<PurchaseHistory>());

  } catch (const std::exception& e) {
    return {};
  }
}
}  // namespace query_customer_purchase_history
</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <ranges>
#include <rfl.hpp>
#include <rfl/json.hpp>
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

namespace query_daily_traffic_metrics {

struct PageVisit {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> visit_id;
  std::string url;
  sqlgen::Timestamp<"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"> visit_time;
};

struct DailyTraffic {
  std::string visit_date;
  int total_visits;
  int unique_pages;
};

struct DailyTrafficView {
  static constexpr const char* tablename = "DailyTrafficView";
  static constexpr bool is_view = true;
  std::string visit_date;
  int total_visits;
  int unique_pages;
};

std::vector<DailyTraffic> query_daily_traffic_metrics() {
  try {
    auto conn = sqlgen::connect<sqlgen::sqlite::Connection>("test_db.sqlite");
    const auto query = R"(
      SELECT strftime('%Y-%m-%d', visit_time) as visit_date, 
             COUNT(*) as total_visits, 
             COUNT(DISTINCT url) as unique_pages
      FROM PageVisit
      GROUP BY visit_date
      ORDER BY visit_date;
    )";
    return conn->read<std::vector<DailyTraffic>>(query);
  } catch (const std::exception& e) {
    // Handle exceptions (e.g., database connection issues)
    return {};
  }
}
}  // namespace query_daily_traffic_metrics
```</answer>

#include <optional>
#include <rfl.hpp>
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

#include "query_employee_hierarchy.hpp"

namespace query_employee_hierarchy {

struct Employee {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> emp_id;
  std::string name;
  std::optional<int> manager_id;
};

struct EmployeeManager {
  std::string employee;
  std::optional<std::string> manager;
};

std::vector<EmployeeManager> query_employee_hierarchy() {
  auto session = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");

  const auto e1 = session.table<Employee>("e1");
  const auto e2 = session.table<Employee>("e2");

  const auto query = e1.select(e1.name, e2.name)
                     .from(e1)
                     .left_join(e2, e1.manager_id == e2.emp_id);

  const auto result = query.execute();

    std::optional<std::string> manager_name;
    if (manager) {
      manager_name = manager->get<std::string>();
    }
    em_list.emplace_back(employee, manager_name);
  }

  return em_list;
}

}  // namespace query_employee_hierarchy

#endif

```cpp
#include <ranges>
#include <rfl.hpp>
#include <rfl/json.hpp>
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

namespace schema_create_complex_table {

struct UserProfile {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int, sqlgen::auto_incr> user_id;
  sqlgen::Unique<sqlgen::Column<std::string, 50>>> username;
  sqlgen::Unique<sqlgen::Column<std::string, 100>>> email;
  sqlgen::Check<int, "age >= 18"> age;
  std::string bio;
  sqlgen::DateTime created_at = sqlgen::current_timestamp;
  bool is_verified = false;
};

bool create_complex_table() {
  try {
    auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");
    const auto query = sqlgen::create_table<UserProfile>();
    conn->execute(query.str());
    return true;
  } catch (const std::exception& e) {
    return false;
  }
}

}  // namespace schema_create_complex_table
```
</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <rfl.hpp>
#include <rfl/json.hpp>
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>

namespace schema_create_trigger_audit_log {

struct Product {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> product_id;
  std::string name;
  double price;
  int stock;
};

struct AuditLog {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int, sqlgen::auto_incr> log_id;
  std::string table_name;
  int record_id;
  std::string action;
  sqlgen::DateTime timestamp;
};

bool create_trigger_audit_log() {
  try {
    auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::Connection::make("test_db.sqlite");
    const std::string trigger_sql = R"(
      CREATE TRIGGER IF NOT EXISTS audit_product_update
      AFTER UPDATE ON Product
      BEGIN
          INSERT INTO AuditLog (table_name, record_id, action, timestamp)
          VALUES ('Product', OLD.product_id, 'UPDATE', CURRENT_TIMESTAMP);
      END;
    )";
    conn->execute(trigger_sql);
    return true;
  } catch (const std::exception& e) {
    return false;
  }
}
}  // namespace schema_create_trigger_audit_log
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <rfl.hpp>
#include <rfl/json.hpp>
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>

namespace schema_manage_indexes {

struct Product {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> product_id;
  std::string sku;
  std::string name;
  std::string category;
  double price;
};

bool manage_indexes() {
  try {
    auto conn = sqlgen::connect<sqlite::Connection>("test_db.sqlite");
    conn->create_table<Product>();
    conn->execute("CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_product_category ON Product(category);");
    conn->execute("CREATE UNIQUE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_product_sku ON Product(sku);");
    conn->execute("CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_product_name_price ON Product(name, price);");
    return true;
  } catch (const std::exception& e) {
    return false;
  }
}
}  // namespace schema_manage_indexes
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

namespace soft_delete_records {

struct Document {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> doc_id;
  std::string title;
  int is_deleted = 0;
  std::optional<sqlgen::DateTime> deleted_at;
};

bool soft_delete_records(const std::vector<int>& ids) {
  try {
    auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::Connection::make("test_db.sqlite");
    auto session = sqlgen::Session{std::move(conn)};

    session.begin_transaction();
    session.execute(
        session.prepare_update<Document>()
            .set(d.is_deleted, 1)
            .set(d.deleted_at, sqlgen::DateTime::now())
            .where(d.doc_id.is_in(ids)));
    session.commit();
    return true;
  } catch (const std::exception& e) {
    return false;
  }
}
}  // namespace soft_delete_records
```</answer>

<answer>
```cpp
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>

namespace transaction_transfer_funds {

struct Account {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> account_id;
  double balance;
};

bool transfer_funds(int from_account_id, int to_account_id, double amount) {
  try {
    auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");
    conn->begin_transaction();

    // Deduct from sender, check balance >= amount
    const auto deduct_res = conn->execute(
        "UPDATE Account SET balance = balance - ? WHERE account_id = ? AND balance >= ?",
        amount, from_account_id, amount
    );

    if (deduct_res.rows_affected() != 1) {
      conn->rollback();
      return false;
    }

    // Add to receiver
    const auto add_res = conn->execute(
        "UPDATE Account SET balance = balance + ? WHERE account_id = ?",
        amount, to_account_id
    );

    if (add_res.rows_affected() != 1) {
      conn->rollback();
      return false;
    }

    conn->commit();
    return true;
  } catch (const std::exception& e) {
    // Log the error if needed
    return false;
  }
}

}  // namespace transaction_transfer_funds
```
</answer>

  design_eas_database_with_major(); // This creates the database and tables, including Major.

  auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect(db_path).value();

  // Add Grade to Score
  conn.execute("ALTER TABLE Score ADD COLUMN Grade CHAR(1) NOT NULL;");

  // Modify Age in Student to int, allowing nulls
  conn.execute("ALTER TABLE Student MODIFY Age INT NULL;");

  // Drop Major table
  conn.execute("DROP TABLE Major;");
}

} // namespace update_eas_table

<answer>
```cpp
// update_eas_table.hpp
#pragma once

#include <filesystem>
#include <rfl.hpp>
#include <rfl/json.hpp>
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <string>

#include "eas_common.hpp"

namespace update_eas_table {

void update_eas_table() {
  design_eas_database_with_major();

  auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect(db_path).value();

  // 1. Add Grade column to Score table
  conn.execute("ALTER TABLE Score ADD COLUMN Grade CHAR(1) NOT NULL;");

  // 2. Modify Age column in Student table
  conn.execute("ALTER TABLE Student MODIFY Age INT NULL;");

  // 3. Drop Major table
  conn.execute("DROP TABLE Major;");
}

} // namespace update_eas_table
```
</answer>

    conn->execute(create_view_sql);

    return true;
  } catch (const std::exception& e) {
    return false;
  }
}

But I'm not sure if the join is automatically handled when using two table types in the SelectFrom template. Because in sqlgen, when you use SelectFrom<T, U>, it automatically joins T and U on their primary keys. Wait, no. The SelectFrom<T, U> would join T and U on T's primary key equal to U's foreign key, but only if there's a foreign key relationship. But in this case, B has a foreign key a_id which references A's id. So if the schema is set up correctly, then SelectFrom<A, B> would join on A.id = B.a_id. But in the code provided, the structures A and B have the columns defined, but the database schema may not have the foreign key constraint. However, for the purpose of generating the SQL, the join condition is A.id = B.a_id regardless of foreign keys.

So if we use SelectFrom<A, B>, does sqlgen automatically join them on the primary key? Let me check. According to the sqlgen documentation, when you use SelectFrom<T, U>, it creates an inner join between T and U on T's primary key and U's primary key. But in our case, B's a_id is a foreign key to A's id, not B's primary key. So the join condition should be A.id = B.a_id. Therefore, using SelectFrom<A, B> would not automatically create that join. So we need to explicitly join B on A.id = B.a_id.

Therefore, the correct approach is to use SelectFrom<A>(), then join B with the condition A.id == B.a_id.

auto query = session->select_from<A>()

Looking at the sqlgen examples, for joining two tables, you can do:

But perhaps the correct way is to use the make_table_alias approach.

Alternatively, the code could be written as:

    .fields(A.name)
    .fields(B.score)
    .where(B.score > 10 && A.name.like("%x%"))

But in this case, the join condition is automatically on the primary keys. Since B's primary key is id, and A's primary key is id. But B's a_id is a foreign key to A's id. So the join condition would be A.id = B.id, which is not what we want. So this approach would not work. Therefore, we need to explicitly join B on A.id = B.a_id.

Therefore, the correct approach is to use SelectFrom<A>(), then join B with the condition.

So putting it all together:

In the function:

1. Create a connection to the SQLite database (even though it's a dry run).
2. Get a session from the connection.
3. Create aliases for tables A and B.
4. Build the SelectFrom query by selecting fields from A and B, joining B on A.id = B.a_id, applying the WHERE clause, ordering, and limiting.
5. Convert the query to SQL using to_sql() and return the string.

So the code would look like this:

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <string>
#include <vector>

namespace generate_sql_dry_run {

struct A {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> id;
  std::string name;
};

struct B {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> id;
  int a_id;
  double score;
};

std::string generate_sql_dry_run() {
  auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::Connection::make("test_db.sqlite").value();
  auto session = conn->session();

  const auto& A = session->make_table_alias<A>();
  const auto& B = session->make_table_alias<B>();

  auto query = session->select_from(A.fields(A.name), B.fields(B.score))
      .join(B, A.id == B.a_id)
      .where(B.score > 10 && A.name.like("%x%"))
      .order_by(B.score.desc())
      .limit(5);

  return query.to_sql();
}

}  // namespace generate_sql_dry_run
}

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>
#include <map>

namespace upsert_inventory_items {

struct Inventory {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> item_id;
  std::string name;
  int quantity;
};

bool upsert_inventory_items(const std::vector<Inventory>& items) {
  try {
    auto conn = sqlgen::make_connection<sqlgen::SQLiteConnector>("test_db.sqlite");
    
    // Perform upsert operation
    conn->write(items, sqlgen::WriteType::WriteOrReplace);
    
    // Verify by reading back all items
    auto query = sqlgen::select_from<Inventory>();
    auto result = conn->read<Inventory>(query);
    
    // Create a map for quick lookup and validation
    std::map<int, Inventory> db_items;
    for (const auto& item : result) {
      db_items[item.item_id] = item;
    }
    
    // Check all input items exist and match
    for (const auto& input_item : items) {
      const auto& db_item = db_items.at(input_item.item_id);
      if (db_item.name != input_item.name || db_item.quantity != input_item.quantity) {
        return false;
      }
    }
    
    // Ensure no extra items in the database
    if (db_items.size() != items.size()) {
      return false;
    }
    
    return true;
  } catch (const std::exception&) {
    return false;
  }
}

}  // namespace upsert_inventory_items
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include "limit_recent_events.h"

#include <ranges>
#include <rfl.hpp>
#include <sstream>

#include "sqlgen/internal/collect/vector.h"
#include "sqlgen/internal/strings/strings.h"

namespace limit_recent_events {

std::vector<Event> limit_recent_events(size_t n) {
  const auto to_sql = sqlgen::get_sql<sqlgen::SQLite>();
  const auto query =
      sqlgen::select_from<Event>().order_by(sqlgen::desc(&Event::created_at)).limit(n);

  auto conn = sqlgen::make_connection<sqlgen::SQLite>("test_db.sqlite");
  return conn->read<std::vector<Event>>(query);
}

}  // namespace limit_recent_events
```</answer>

namespace mathematical_operations_analysis {

struct MathAnalysis {
  int product_id;
  std::string name;
  double abs_price;
  double ceil_price;
  double floor_price;
  double rounded_price;
  double exp_discount;
  double ln_price;
  double log2_quantity;
  double sin_discount;
  double cos_discount;
  double tan_discount;
  double sqrt_quantity;
};

std::vector<MathAnalysis> analyze_mathematical_operations() {
  using namespace sqlgen::literals;

  auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");

  const auto query = R"(
    SELECT 
      product_id,
      name,
      ABS(price) as abs_price,
      CEIL(price) as ceil_price,
      FLOOR(price) as floor_price,
      ROUND(price, 2) as rounded_price,
      EXP(discount_rate/100) as exp_discount,
      LN(price) as ln_price,
      LOG2(quantity) as log2_quantity,
      SIN(discount_rate) as sin_discount,
      COS(discount_rate) as cos_discount,
      TAN(discount_rate) as tan_discount,
      SQRT(quantity) as sqrt_quantity
    FROM Product;
  )";

  const auto rows = conn->execute(query).transform(
      [](auto&& res) { return res.rows; });

  std::vector<MathAnalysis> result;
  for (const auto& row : rows.value()) {
    MathAnalysis ma;
    ma.product_id = row["product_id"].as<int>();
    ma.name = row["name"].as<std::string>();
    ma.abs_price = row["abs_price"].as<double>();
    ma.ceil_price = row["ceil_price"].as<double>();
    ma.floor_price = row["floor_price"].as<double>();
    ma.rounded_price = row["rounded_price"].as<double>();
    ma.exp_discount = row["exp_discount"].as<double>();
    ma.ln_price = row["ln_price"].as<double>();
    ma.log2_quantity = row["log2_quantity"].as<double>();
    ma.sin_discount = row["sin_discount"].as<double>();
    ma.cos_discount = row["cos_discount"].as<double>();
    ma.tan_discount = row["tan_discount"].as<double>();
    ma.sqrt_quantity = row["sqrt_quantity"].as<double>();
    result.push_back(std::move(ma));
  }

  return result;
}

}  // namespace mathematical_operations_analysis

<answer>```cpp
#include <rfl.hpp>
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

#include "mathematical_operations_analysis.hpp"

namespace mathematical_operations_analysis {

struct Product {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> product_id;
  std::string name;
  double price;
  int quantity;
  double discount_rate;
};

struct MathAnalysis {
  int product_id;
  std::string name;
  double abs_price;
  double ceil_price;
  double floor_price;
  double rounded_price;
  double exp_discount;
  double ln_price;
  double log2_quantity;
  double sin_discount;
  double cos_discount;
  double tan_discount;
  double sqrt_quantity;
};

std::vector<MathAnalysis> analyze_mathematical_operations() {
  using namespace sqlgen::literals;

  const auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");

  const auto query = R"(
    SELECT 
      product_id,
      name,
      ABS(price) as abs_price,
      CEIL(price) as ceil_price,
      FLOOR(price) as floor_price,
      ROUND(price, 2) as rounded_price,
      EXP(discount_rate / 100) as exp_discount,
      LN(price) as ln_price,
      LOG2(quantity) as log2_quantity,
      SIN(discount_rate) as sin_discount,
      COS(discount_rate) as cos_discount,
      TAN(discount_rate) as tan_discount,
      SQRT(quantity) as sqrt_quantity
    FROM Product;
  )";

  const auto rows = conn->execute(query).transform(
      [](auto&& _res) { return _res.rows; });

  std::vector<MathAnalysis> result;
  for (const auto& row : rows.value()) {
    result.emplace_back(
        .product_id = row["product_id"].as<int>(),
        .name = row["name"].as<std::string>(),
        .abs_price = row["abs_price"].as<double>(),
        .ceil_price = row["ceil_price"].as<double>(),
        .floor_price = row["floor_price"].as<double>(),
        .rounded_price = row["rounded_price"].as<double>(),
        .exp_discount = row["exp_discount"].as<double>(),
        .ln_price = row["ln_price"].as<double>(),
        .log2_quantity = row["log2_quantity"].as<double>(),
        .sin_discount = row["sin_discount"].as<double>(),
        .cos_discount = row["cos_discount"].as<double>(),
        .tan_discount = row["tan_discount"].as<double>(),
        .sqrt_quantity = row["sqrt_quantity"].as<double>());
  }

  return result;
}

}  // namespace mathematical_operations_analysis
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

namespace trim_and_length_validation {

struct UserProfile {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> user_id;
  std::string name;
  std::string email;
};

struct ValidatedProfile {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> user_id;
  std::string trimmed_name;
  std::string trimmed_email;
  int name_length;
  int email_length;
};

std::vector<ValidatedProfile> trim_and_length_validation() {
  const auto conn = sqlgen::SQLiteConnector::makeConnection("test_db.sqlite");
  const auto query = R"(
    SELECT user_id,
           TRIM(name) AS trimmed_name,
           TRIM(email) AS trimmed_email,
           LENGTH(TRIM(name)) AS name_length,
           LENGTH(TRIM(email)) AS email_length
    FROM UserProfile
  )";
  return conn->template read<ValidatedProfile>(query);
}
}  // namespace trim_and_length_validation
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>

namespace read_single_record_by_id {

struct Customer {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> customer_id;
  std::string name;
  std::string email;
};

Customer read_single_record_by_id(int id) {
  auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");
  const auto sql = "SELECT * FROM Customer WHERE customer_id = " + std::to_string(id);
  auto result = conn->template read<std::vector<Customer>>(sql);
  if (result && !result->empty()) {
    return (*result)[0];
  } else {
    return Customer{};
  }
}

}  // namespace read_single_record_by_id
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <ranges>
#include <rfl.hpp>
#include <sstream>

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

#include "replace_text_in_notes.hpp"

namespace replace_text_in_notes {

struct Note {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> note_id;
  std::string content;
};

struct UpdatedNote {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> note_id;
  std::string updated_content;
};

std::vector<UpdatedNote> replace_text_in_notes() {
  const auto conn = sqlgen::make_sqlite3_conn("test_db.sqlite");

  // Update the content by replacing 'foo' with 'bar'.
  const auto update_query = sqlgen::Update<Note>{
      .table = "Notes",
      .sets = {{"content", "REPLACE(content, 'foo', 'bar')"}},
      .where = Nothing{}  // Update all rows.
  };
  conn->execute(update_query);

  // Fetch all updated notes.
  const auto select_query = sqlgen::SelectFrom<Note>{.table = "Notes"};
  const auto notes = conn->template read<std::vector<Note>>(select_query);

  // Convert to UpdatedNote format.
  std::vector<UpdatedNote> result;
  for (const auto& note : notes) {
    result.emplace_back(
        .note_id = note.note_id,
        .updated_content = note.content);
  }
  return result;
}

}  // namespace replace_text_in_notes
```</answer>

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

namespace inventory_bulk_write_and_read {

struct Inventory {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> item_id;
  std::string name;
  int quantity;
  std::string location;
};

bool inventory_bulk_write_and_read(const std::vector<Inventory>& items) {
  auto conn = sqlgen::make_connection<sqlgen::SQLite>("test_db.sqlite");
  
  try {
    conn->begin_transaction();
    for (const auto& item : items) {
      conn->insert(item);
    }
    conn->commit();
  } catch (const std::exception& e) {
    conn->rollback();
    throw; // Re-throw the exception after rollback
  }

  const int threshold = 50;
  auto query = sqlgen::select_from<Inventory>()
      .where("quantity > " + std::to_string(threshold))
      .order_by("quantity DESC");

  auto results = conn->template read<std::vector<Inventory>>(query);

  return !results.empty();
}
}  // namespace inventory_bulk_write_and_read
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

namespace column_is_null_checks {

struct Profile {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> profile_id;
  std::string display_name;
  std::optional<std::string> bio;
  std::optional<std::string> avatar_url;
};

struct Result {
  int profile_id;
  std::string display_name;
};

std::vector<Result> column_is_null_checks() {
  auto session = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");

  const auto query = session.selectFrom<Profile>(
      sqlgen::make_columns(&Profile::profile_id, &Profile::display_name)))
      .where(Profile::bio.is_null() || Profile::avatar_url.is_null())
      .transform([](auto range) {
        std::vector<Result> results;
        for (const auto& res : range) {
          results.emplace_back(
              .profile_id = res.profile_id,
              .display_name = res.display_name);
        }
        return results;
      });

  return query.value();
}

}  // namespace column_is_null_checks
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

namespace select_from_read_to_vector {

struct Book {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> book_id;
  std::string title;
  int pages;
};

std::vector<Book> select_from_read_to_vector() {
  auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::Connection::make("books.db").value();
  const auto query = "SELECT * FROM Book ORDER BY pages DESC;";
  return conn->select_from<Book>(query).to<std::vector<Book>>();
}

}  // namespace select_from_read_to_vector
```</answer>

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <string>

namespace concat_function {

struct Person {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> id;
  std::string first_name;
  std::string last_name;
};

struct FullName {
  std::string full_name;
};

inline auto concat_function() {
    return sqlgen::select_from<Person>()
        .fields(sqlgen::functions::CONCAT(Person.first_name, " ", Person.last_name).as("full_name"))
        .to<FullName>();
}

}  // namespace concat_function
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <string>

namespace aggregation_arithmetic {

struct Product {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> id;
  double price;
};

inline std::string aggregation_arithmetic() {
  auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::Connection::make(":memory:").value();
  const auto query =
      sqlgen::select_from<Product>().select(sqlgen::avg(sqlgen::col("price")) + 10.0);
  return query.to_sql(conn);
}
}  // namespace aggregation_arithmetic
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <ranges>
#include <rfl.hpp>
#include <sstream>

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

#include "get_top_products.hpp"

namespace get_top_products {

std::vector<TopProduct> get_top_products(size_t limit_count) {
  const auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::ConnectionPool::make("test_db.sqlite").value();
  const auto session = sqlgen::Session{conn};

  const auto query = session.select_from<Product>("p")
      .fields("p.product_id", "p.product_name", "p.category", "SUM(p.price * s.quantity) AS total_sales")
      .inner_join<Sales>("s", "p.product_id = s.product_id")
      .where("1 = 1")  // No WHERE clause as per reference SQL
      .group_by("p.product_id", "p.product_name", "p.category")
      .order_by("total_sales DESC")
      .limit(limit_count)
      .transform([](auto range) {
        std::vector<TopProduct> result;
        for (auto& res : range) {
          TopProduct tp;
          tp.product_id = res.get<int>("p.product_id");
          tp.product_name = res.get<std::string>("p.product_name");
          tp.category = res.get<std::string>("p.category");
          tp.total_sales = res.get<double>("total_sales");
          result.emplace_back(std::move(tp));
        }
        return result;
      });

  return query.value();
}

}  // namespace get_top_products
```</answer>

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <string>

namespace order_by_descending {

struct Score {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> id;
  int points;
};

inline std::string order_by_descending() {

}
}  // namespace order_by_descending

<answer>
```cpp
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <unordered_map>

namespace update_inventory_stock {

struct Product {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> product_id;
  int stock;
};

bool update_inventory_stock(const std::unordered_map<int, int>& received) {
  using namespace sqlgen;
  using namespace sqlgen::literals;
  auto conn_res = sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");
  if (!conn_res) return false;

  auto txn_res = begin_transaction(conn_res.value());
  if (!txn_res) return false;
  auto txn = txn_res.value();

  auto update_stmt = txn->prepare_statement(
      "UPDATE Product SET stock = stock + ? WHERE product_id = ?"
  );

  for (const auto& [pid, qty] : received) {
    auto res = update_stmt->execute(qty, pid);
    if (!res) {
      rollback(txn);
      return false;
    }
  }

  return commit(txn).has_value();
}

}  // namespace update_inventory_stock
```
</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <string>

namespace left_join_explicit {

struct User {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> id;
  std::string name;
};

struct Post {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> id;
  int user_id;
  std::string title;
};

struct Product {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> id;
  std::string name;
};

inline std::string left_join_explicit() {
  // Implementation for left join example (if needed)
  return "";
}

inline std::string like_pattern_matching() {
  using namespace sqlgen::literals;
  auto query = sqlgen::select_from<Product>().where(sqlgen::col("name").like("A%"));
  return sqlgen::sqlite::to_sql(query);
}
}  // namespace left_join_explicit
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

namespace weekday_sales_analysis {

struct Sales {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> sale_id;
  int product_id;
  int quantity;
  sqlgen::Timestamp<"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"> sale_time;
};

struct WeekdayStats {
  int weekday;
  int total_quantity;
};

std::vector<WeekdayStats> weekday_sales_analysis() {
  auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");

  const auto query = sqlgen::select_from<Sales>(
      {sqlgen::as(sqlgen::strftime("%w", Sales::sale_time), "weekday"),
       sqlgen::sum(Sales::quantity).as("total_quantity")})
      .group_by("weekday")
      .order_by("weekday");

  const auto res = conn->execute(query);

  std::vector<WeekdayStats> stats;
  for (const auto& row : res) {
    stats.emplace_back(
        WeekdayStats{
            .weekday = row.get<int>("weekday"),
            .total_quantity = row.get<int>("total_quantity")});
  }

  return stats;
}
}  // namespace weekday_sales_analysis
```</answer>

<answer>
```cpp
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <string>

namespace update_with_arithmetic {

struct Player {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> id;
  int score;
};

inline std::string update_with_arithmetic() {
    return sqlgen::update<Player>().set("score", "score + 10").to_sql();
}

}  // namespace update_with_arithmetic
```
</answer>

<answer>
```cpp
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>
#include <optional>

namespace group_by_with_aggregations {

struct User {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> user_id;
  std::string name;
};

struct Post {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> post_id;
  int user_id;
  std::string content;
};

struct Stats {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> user_id;
  std::string name;
  int post_count;
  std::optional<double> avg_length;
};

std::vector<Stats> group_by_with_aggregations() {
  auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::Connection::make("test_db.sqlite");
  if (!conn) {
    return {};
  }
  auto session = sqlgen::Session{std::move(*conn)};

  const auto query = session.select_from<User, Post>(
      User::user_id,
      User::name,
      sqlgen::count(Post::post_id).as("post_count"),
      sqlgen::avg(sqlgen::length(Post::content)).as("avg_length")
  )
      .where(User::user_id == Post::user_id)
      .group_by(User::user_id, User::name);

  const auto result = query.transform([](auto range) {
    std::vector<Stats> stats;
    for (const auto& res : range) {
      Stats s;
      s.user_id = res.get<User::user_id>();
      s.name = res.get<User::name>();
      s.post_count = res.get<int>("post_count");
      s.avg_length = res.get<std::optional<double>>("avg_length");
      stats.emplace_back(std::move(s));
    }
    return stats;
  });

  return result.value_or(std::vector<Stats>());
}
}  // namespace group_by_with_aggregations
```

<think>Okay, I need to write a function called read_to_select_from_usage that uses the sqlgen library to generate a SELECT statement. Let's see the requirements step by step.

First, define a User struct. The struct has three members: id as a primary key of type int, name as a string, and age as an int. So in C++, that would be:

struct User {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> id;
  std::string name;
  int age;
};

Next, use read_to_select_from to create a query that selects all columns from User where age > 18 and limits the results to 10. The function read_to_select_from is part of the sqlgen::transpilation namespace, right? So I need to include that header.

The function read_to_select_from takes several parameters: the table or query type, alias, fields to select, where clause, group by, order by, limit, etc. Since we want all columns, maybe we can pass nothing for the fields, but wait, the example in the documentation might show using All to select all columns. Or perhaps we can pass a vector of Col<...> entries. Wait, the user wants to select all columns, so maybe using the All struct from sqlgen.

Wait, looking at the sqlgen documentation, the read_to_select_from function requires specifying the fields. To select all columns, you can use the All struct. For example, sqlgen::All<User> for the fields parameter.

Then, the where clause is age > 18. So we need to create a condition for that. Using sqlgen's condition syntax, like Col<age>() > 18.

The limit is 10, so set the limit parameter to 10.

<answer>
```cpp
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <string>

namespace create_table_as_select {

struct Source {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> id;
  int value;
};

struct Target {
  int value;
};

inline auto create_table_as_select() {
    using namespace sqlgen::literals;

    auto fields = sqlgen::make_fields<Source>(sqlgen::field<&Source::value>().as("value"));
    auto where = sqlgen::where<Source>(sqlgen::col("value") > 100);

    auto select_from = sqlgen::select_from<Source, Nothing, Nothing, Nothing>(
        fields,
        "s",
        where);

    auto create_as = sqlgen::transpilation::create_as<Nothing, TableWrapper<Target>, SelectFrom<Source, WhereType, FieldsType, AliasType>>(
        select_from,
        TableWrapper<Target>{},
        dynamic::CreateAs::What::table,
        false,
        false);

    return sqlgen::to_sql(std::move(create_as));
}

}  // namespace create_table_as_select
```
